2604.13991.title: Adaptive Conformal Prediction for Improving Factuality of Generations by Large Language Models
2604.13991.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are prone to generating factually incorrect outputs. Recent work has applied conformal prediction to provide uncertainty estimates and statistical guarantees for the factuality of LLM generations. However, existing approaches are typically not prompt-adaptive, limiting their ability to capture input-dependent variability. As a result, they may filter out too few items (leading to over-coverage) or too many (under-coverage) for a given task or prompt. We propose an adaptive conformal prediction approach that extends conformal score transformation methods to LLMs, with applications to long-form generation and multiple-choice question answering. This enables prompt-dependent calibration, retaining marginal coverage guarantees while improving conditional coverage. In addition, the approach naturally supports selective prediction, allowing unreliable claims or answer choices to be filtered out in downstream applications. We evaluate our approach on multiple white-box models across diverse domains and show that it significantly outperforms existing baselines in terms of conditional coverage.
2604.13991.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）容易產生事實上不正確的輸出。最近的研究應用符合預測來提供不確定性估計和統計保證，以確保LLM生成的事實性。然而，現有的方法通常不具備提示自適應性，限制了它們捕捉依賴於輸入的變異性的能力。因此，它們可能對於特定任務或提示過濾出太少的項目（導致過度覆蓋）或太多的項目（不足覆蓋）。我們提出了一種自適應符合預測方法，將符合分數轉換方法擴展到LLMs，應用於長篇生成和多選題回答。這使得提示依賴的校準成為可能，保留邊際覆蓋保證，同時改善條件覆蓋。此外，該方法自然支持選擇性預測，允許在下游應用中過濾掉不可靠的聲明或答案選擇。我們在多個白盒模型上進行了評估，涵蓋多個領域，並顯示出在條件覆蓋方面顯著優於現有基準。
2604.13991.author: Aleksandr Rubashevskii et.al.
2604.13991.authors: Aleksandr Rubashevskii, Dzianis Piatrashyn, Preslav Nakov, Maxim Panov
